metadata.title: Breeder profile web research - July 15 batch 6 F
metadata.notes: Public-web research only. Breeder, employer, applicant, assignee, holder, licensee, commercial brand, and current successor roles remain distinct. Candidate-parent fields indicate a supported affiliation or operating relationship, not a blanket legal rollup. No external communications were made.

profiles.ownershipType: individual - retired corporate breeder without personal title
profiles.ownershipSummary: Keith S. Warren is the retired Director of New Product Development at J. Frank Schmidt & Son Co. The cited JFS-KW2QX patent names Warren as inventor and documents assignment to J. Frank Schmidt & Son Co.; no evidence supports personal ownership of the associated JFS portfolio.
profiles.candidateParent: J. Frank Schmidt & Son Co. for cited JFS records
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official company history identifies Warren's 40-year JFS breeding career, and the cited patent records assignment to JFS. Apply the affiliation at record level because employment alone is not proof that JFS owns every historical Warren record.
profiles.headquarters: 9500 SE 327th Avenue, Boring, Oregon 97009, United States (J. Frank Schmidt & Son Co.)
profiles.leadershipSummary: Warren is retired. JFS states that Guy Meacham now manages New Plant Development, while second- and third-generation Schmidt family members govern the company, providing documented operating succession.
profiles.primaryContactName: J. Frank Schmidt & Son Co. Customer Service
profiles.trademarkStatus: official brand-use evidence; registry ownership not independently checked

webResearchSources.label: JFS company history, Warren retirement, and program succession
JFS-KW2QX patent and assignment to J. Frank Schmidt & Son Co.

profiles.webResearchNotes: Corporate employee profile with no personal title evidence. Preserve cultivar-level assignments and do not treat post-retirement inventor credit as a current employment role.

profiles.ownershipType: individual - current public-university professor and former public-institution breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Nobuhiro Kotoda worked at NARO and its fruit-tree institute lineage through June 2013 and has worked at Saga University since July 2013, becoming professor in April 2023. His legacy apple breeding records are NARO institutional outputs; his later Saga employment is not evidence that Saga acquired those rights.
profiles.headquarters: Saga University, Saga, Japan (current employment); NARO headquarters, Tsukuba, Ibaraki, Japan (legacy rights administration)
profiles.leadershipSummary: This is a completed employment transition from NARO to Saga University, not a transfer of the legacy cultivar portfolio. NARO retains a formal breeder-rights licensing function, and no personal succession or private rightsholder vehicle was found.

webResearchSources.label: J-GLOBAL current Saga University role and work history
NARO variety-rights licensing route
NARO breeder-rights licensing procedure

profiles.webResearchNotes: Public-institution and public-university profile. Keep the pre-July-2013 NARO portfolio separate from current Saga University research.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Takashi Kimura is documented in NARO's Kyushu Okinawa strawberry breeding program. NARO identifies him as one of the breeders of Koiminori, and the 2025 cultivar paper lists his NARO affiliation; no personal title or private breeding company was found.
profiles.headquarters: NARO Kyushu Okinawa Agricultural Research Center, Japan; exact current duty station not independently confirmed
profiles.leadershipSummary: The supported strawberry work is team-based and institutionally continued at NARO. An authoritative personal title or named successor for Kimura was not found, but the 2025 publication continues to list him with NARO.

webResearchSources.label: NARO Koiminori breeder profile
2025 Koiminori cultivar paper and NARO affiliation
NARO breeder-rights licensing procedure

profiles.webResearchNotes: The exact identity is supported for NARO strawberry records. Apple and cherry rows in the aggregated dashboard profile were not corroborated and should be split before any institutional rollup.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Toshio Takahara is a historical Japanese public-sector citrus breeder associated with the Fruit Tree Research Station and NARO lineage. The Tsunokaori patent names him as inventor and records assignment to the Fruit Tree Research Station, Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries, rather than personal ownership.
profiles.headquarters: Historical citrus work at the Kuchinotsu fruit-tree station in Nagasaki, Japan; current rights inquiries through NARO in Tsukuba, Ibaraki, Japan
profiles.leadershipSummary: Takahara's documented work is institutional and historical. Later publications acknowledge him as a former specialist contributor, but no authoritative current employment role was found; NARO's citrus group and licensing office provide institutional continuity.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Institutional contact for NARO-lineage fruit varieties

webResearchSources.label: Tsunokaori patent, inventor list, and public-station assignment
NARO citrus cultivar repository record naming Takahara
NARO breeder-rights licensing procedure

profiles.webResearchNotes: Public-program breeder, not a personal-rights target. The pineapple records also arise from public breeding activity, but each filing-era institution should be retained at record level.

profiles.ownershipSummary: The grape breeder abbreviated as M. Ivanov resolves to Miroslav Ivanov of the Institute of Viticulture and Enology - Pleven. The official Trapezitsa page and Agricultural Academy journal align on institute, coauthors, crop, and cultivar history; the abbreviation is not a company or standalone rightsholder.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Ivanov remains associated with the public Institute of Viticulture and Enology in recent research. No private breeding vehicle, personal ownership claim, or named individual successor was found; continuity is institutional.
profiles.primaryContactName: Institute of Viticulture and Enology - Pleven

webResearchSources.label: Official Institute Trapezitsa cultivar page naming M. Ivanov
Agricultural Academy article identifying Miroslav Ivanov and institute
Institute public contact path

profiles.webResearchNotes: Resolved only for the Pleven grape records. Do not merge records for M. Ivanova or unrelated Ivanov researchers into this profile.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Ibergen Frutales S.L. is a Spanish family company developing interspecific plum-apricot and baby-plum varieties. Spain's official 2024 grant records name Ibergen Frutales S.L. as title holder for IBP ONE and IBP TEN.
profiles.headquarters: Breeding operations in San Rafael del Rio, Castellon, Spain, with a second trial site in Caspe, Zaragoza, Spain
profiles.leadershipSummary: David Nogueroles Barkley and his brother Juan are publicly identified as co-owners; Juan's breeding experience launched the program. No next-generation or management successor was publicly identified, leaving a founder-family succession signal.
profiles.trademarkStatus: IberPlum and BabyPlum reported as internationally registered marks; registry ownership not independently checked

webResearchSources.label: Ibergen official company, portfolio, facilities, and contact
Spanish official grants for IBP ONE and IBP TEN
Co-owner interview and IberPlum/BabyPlum brand evidence

profiles.webResearchNotes: Independent family-company profile. Verify the legal cap table, title for each IBP/BBP selection, and territorial licenses before consolidation.

profiles.ownershipType: individual - independent private apple breeder and variety-company partner
profiles.ownershipSummary: Jean-Luc Carrieres is a private apple breeder in Montcuq, France, and a member of the Red Moon collaboration. The RM-1 patent documents assignment through Benoit Escande Editions to Red Moon GmbH, while official Red Moon material assigns Xeleven variety and SWING rights to the Red Moon company; ownership is cultivar specific rather than personal by default.
profiles.candidateParent: Red Moon GmbH / Red Moon Company, record dependent
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Carrieres is a documented Red Moon member, and cited patent and company materials place RM-1 and Xeleven rights with Red Moon entities. This does not prove a blanket transfer of every Carrieres selection.
profiles.headquarters: Breeder activity in Montcuq-en-Quercy-Blanc, Lot, France; Red Moon company contact historically in Bolzano, Italy
profiles.leadershipSummary: Carrieres remains the named breeder within a multi-party platform involving Escande, KIKU Variety Management/Braun, and other partners. No named personal successor was found; commercial continuity is organized through the Red Moon company and its partners.
profiles.trademarkStatus: official rights material attributes SWING and related variety/trademark rights to Red Moon Company; current registry ownership not independently checked

webResearchSources.label: RM-1 patent and assignment chain to Red Moon GmbH
Official Australian PBR journal naming Carrieres and Red Moon holder
Red Moon official site for Xeleven and SWING

profiles.webResearchNotes: Keep breeder credit, patent assignee, PBR holder, and trademark owner separate. Red Moon is a supported portfolio relationship, not a universal parent for all Carrieres records.

profiles.ownershipType: individual - retired public-research breeder and CREA associate
profiles.ownershipSummary: Giuseppe Russo is the Acireale citrus researcher tied to CREA and its predecessor programs. CREA's official cultivar catalog identifies CREA as title holder for SUN RED, and a 2023 CREA board resolution describes Russo as a retired former employee associated with CREA-OFA; the rights are institutional, not personal.
profiles.headquarters: CREA-OFA, Corso Savoia 190, 95024 Acireale, Sicily, Italy; CREA legal headquarters in Rome, Italy
profiles.leadershipSummary: Russo is retired from employee status but formally associated with CREA-OFA. Current cultivar management and licensing sit with CREA's institutional structure; no personal successor or private successor company was found.

webResearchSources.label: CREA Acireale laboratory and public contact information
CREA board resolution describing Russo as retired and associated
CREA plant-innovation catalog with SUN RED ownership and breeders

profiles.webResearchNotes: Public-institution licensing profile. Some older CREA pages still list Russo as an operational contact; the 2023 formal retirement/association record controls the current-employment description.

profiles.ownershipType: malformed duplicate - parser fragment of a strategic-scale company
profiles.ownershipSummary: S.L. Viveros is not a separately verified person or company. It is a malformed fragment produced from the full legal entity COMERCIAL TECNICA Y VIVEROS, S.L., which trades as COTEVISA and is named in official Spanish variety records.
profiles.candidateParent: COMERCIAL TECNICA Y VIVEROS, S.L. / COTEVISA
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The official company site expands COTEVISA to Comercial Tecnica y Viveros S.L., and Spanish registry material repeats that exact full entity. The supplied string is a duplicate parser fragment, not an independent owner.
profiles.headquarters: Carretera Nacional 340, km 873.5, 46250 L'Alcudia, Valencia, Spain
profiles.leadershipSummary: The official site presents a 30-plus-year operating company with up to 12 million plants of annual capacity and more than 10 hectares of greenhouses, but does not name current ownership or a successor. The scale and malformed-duplicate status make this entry non-actionable as a standalone profile.

webResearchSources.label: COTEVISA official site and full legal name
Spanish Ministry entity record for the full company
Spanish variety bulletin naming the full company and Del Pilar

profiles.webResearchNotes: Deduplicate into the full COTEVISA legal entity. Do not create a separate owner, leadership record, or portfolio for S.L. Viveros.

profiles.ownershipType: individual - corporate strawberry breeder without a public personal title
profiles.ownershipSummary: Jonathan R. Nelson is a California strawberry breeder on recurring Fragaria Plant Sciences B.V. filings. The FPS-15.2016-31 patent records assignment by Jonathan, Daniel, Scott, and Jeffrey Nelson to Fragaria Plant Sciences B.V.; no personal ownership or public executive title for Jonathan was found.
profiles.candidateParent: Fragaria Plant Sciences B.V. for cited FPS records
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Recent patents and PBR filings consistently connect Nelson to Fragaria Plant Sciences B.V. and document assignment for the cited cultivars. The evidence does not establish that the company owns every historical Nelson record.
profiles.headquarters: Breeder activity in Watsonville, California, United States; Fragaria Plant Sciences B.V. holder address in Veldhoven, Netherlands
profiles.leadershipSummary: Four recurring Nelson co-breeders provide a multi-person breeding bench, but no official public source establishes their family relationship, management titles, or a succession plan. Jonathan should be treated as a corporate breeder, not a personal acquisition target.
profiles.primaryContactName: Fragaria Plant Sciences B.V. rights record
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Public PBR holder and agent path

webResearchSources.label: FPS-15.2016-31 patent and assignment to Fragaria Plant Sciences B.V.
Canadian PBR holder, breeder, and agent record
Fragaria Plant Sciences B.V. assigned-patent portfolio

profiles.webResearchNotes: The Canadian record misspells the first name as Johnathon; the U.S. patent uses Jonathan R. Nelson and records the assignment. Preserve filing-specific assignee and holder details.

profiles.ownershipType: individual - private fruit breeder, company director, and shareholder
profiles.ownershipSummary: The abbreviated J. McLaren resolves to New Zealand fruit breeder John McLaren through the Nevson PBR and patent records. Historical records identify him with Southern Trees Limited, while cultivar rights such as Nevson sit with Nevis Fruit Company Limited; current registry-derived data still lists John McLaren as a Nevis director and shareholder.
profiles.candidateParent: Nevis Fruit Company Limited, record dependent
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The Nevson patent documents assignment to Nevis Fruit Company Limited, and current registry-derived data identifies John McLaren as an active director and shareholder. Apply the rollup only where the cultivar record names Nevis.
profiles.headquarters: Breeder activity in Alexandra/Bannockburn, Central Otago, New Zealand; Nevis registered office in Cromwell, New Zealand
profiles.leadershipSummary: Current registry-derived data lists John McLaren and Pierre Riou as directors. John and Gillian McLaren jointly hold 25 percent, with other shares held by Riou, Innovar S.A.R.L., and additional investors; no named successor to McLaren's breeding role was found.
profiles.primaryContactName: New Zealand Companies Register entry for Nevis Fruit Company Limited
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Official company-record path; no public business email or phone located

webResearchSources.label: Canadian Nevson PBR naming John McLaren, Southern Trees, and Nevis holder
Nevson patent and assignment to Nevis Fruit Company Limited
Current registry-derived Nevis directors and shareholders

profiles.webResearchNotes: Southern Trees is the historical breeder affiliation shown on Nevson, not evidence of current portfolio ownership. Verify each Nevis, Southern Trees, and personal filing separately.

profiles.ownershipType: individual - private founder, chairman, and grape breeder with family succession
profiles.ownershipSummary: Tomio Shimura worked for Mann's Wine for 34 years before founding Shimura Grape Research Institute in 1986. The current official company site identifies him as chairman and founder and identifies his son, Akio Shimura (also rendered Kosei from the same Japanese name), as the representative leading the operating company.
profiles.candidateParent: Shimura Grape Research Institute Co., Ltd., record dependent
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The company identifies Tomio as founder/chairman and his son as current representative, but no source reviewed proves that every historical Tomio Shimura variety right was transferred into the incorporated company.
profiles.leadershipSummary: A documented father-to-son succession has occurred. The son took over operations and incorporated the institute in March 2023; the official site now lists Tomio as chairman and Akio as representative, while Tomio remains the founder and senior technical figure.
profiles.trademarkStatus: official store uses a registered-mark symbol for Fuji no Kagayaki; registry ownership not independently checked

webResearchSources.label: Official company history, roles, address, and contact
Father-son succession and March 2023 incorporation
Official store and Fuji no Kagayaki brand-use evidence

profiles.webResearchNotes: Romanization of the current representative's given name varies between Akio and Kosei in English material. Preserve cultivar-level title until assignment evidence is obtained.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Hae-Sung Hwang is a former breeder with Korea's Rural Development Administration and National Institute of Horticultural and Herbal Science. The Hanareum PBR names RDA as applicant and holder, not Hwang personally; a Korean Society for Horticultural Science notice records his mandatory retirement on June 30, 2019.
profiles.headquarters: Current institutional contact at NIHHS, 100 Nongsaengmyeong-ro, Iseo-myeon, Wanju-gun, Jeollabuk-do, Republic of Korea; historical work also associated with Suwon and the pear program
profiles.leadershipSummary: Hwang entered retirement preparation in July 2018 and mandatory retirement in June 2019. RDA/NIHHS maintains a team-based public breeding program, so rights administration and succession are institutional rather than personal.
profiles.primaryContactName: National Institute of Horticultural and Herbal Science

webResearchSources.label: Canadian Hanareum PBR naming RDA holder and Hwang breeder
AGRIS pear-breeding publication record
NIHHS official institutional contact

profiles.webResearchNotes: Retired public-program breeder, not an individual acquisition target. Do not use historical personal email or mobile details as current contact information.

profiles.ownershipType: deceased independent kiwifruit breeder with a record-split transferred portfolio
profiles.ownershipSummary: Donald A. (Don) Skelton was an independent New Zealand kiwifruit breeder. SNZI states that it acquired the breeding business and worldwide rights to about 42 Skelton varieties on January 1, 2018, but also confirms that several varieties had previously been sold; the Skelton A19 patent, for example, is assigned to Enza/Enzafruit rather than SNZI.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: SNZI officially describes its 2018 acquisition of the business and a large block of Skelton rights, while its own history and patent records show excluded or previously sold cultivars. No blanket portfolio rollup is supportable.
profiles.headquarters: Historical breeding activity in Waiuku, Rangiriri, and Huntly, New Zealand; SNZI contact at 54 Alexander Avenue, Auckland 0630, New Zealand
profiles.leadershipSummary: Skelton is reported deceased. SNZI's Allan Rutledge now leads the acquired-rights platform, which states that it eliminated 14 varieties after review, sold some territorial rights, and prepared the remaining business for sale, creating an explicit portfolio-transition signal.
profiles.trademarkStatus: official historical brand-use evidence; registry ownership and current holder vary by brand and were not independently checked

webResearchSources.label: SNZI acquisition, portfolio review, exclusions, and sale preparation
Skelton A19 patent and assignment to Enza/Enzafruit
Public report identifying the late Don Skelton

profiles.webResearchNotes: High-priority chain-of-title and sale-process diligence. Map SNZI, Enzafruit, Bostock, territorial buyers, expired rights, and any estate interests cultivar by cultivar.

profiles.ownershipType: private family-owned international apple breeding and variety-management company
profiles.ownershipSummary: Zouk b.v. is a Belgian company that breeds, protects, and manages apple varieties and launches them with international partners. It is a sister company to the Nicolai family nursery, not an individual breeder; the public sources do not disclose a complete legal cap table.
profiles.headquarters: Brustem Industriepark, Lichtenberglaan 2050, B-3800 Sint-Truiden, Belgium
profiles.leadershipSummary: Third-generation siblings Laura, Vincent, and Michiel Nicolai operate the family businesses. Laura focuses on genetics and breeding, while founder-generation Johan Nicolai is officially retired but remains involved. Zouk reports an international network reaching 14 countries and 63 million in annual production, supporting strategic-scale classification.
profiles.trademarkStatus: official trade-name use; registry ownership not independently checked

webResearchSources.label: Zouk official company scope, family-company status, scale, and contact
Zouk protected cultivars and commercial trade names
Nicolai third-generation leadership and sister-company relationship

profiles.webResearchNotes: Strategic-scale relationship or licensing profile rather than a small standalone acquisition target. Verify Zouk's cap table, sister-company agreements, variety title, trademark title, and partner territories separately.
